what_changed: registry-security-team declared a Code Signing Readiness Review and issued a 30-day recommendation for mandatory package-signing enforcement, triggered by the strutil v2.1.3 supply chain compromise affecting 84,000 downstream consumers
board-security-committee constituted governance-working-group under foundation-committee-charter article 12.4 with authority to draft and propose mandatory code-signing policy within 14 days
governance-working-group published PROPOSAL-CSRP-001 requiring GPG-signed releases from verified accounts; pre-policy releases grandfathered; enforcement deadline set to 2026-03-15
community-governance-process translated PROPOSAL-CSRP-001 from a working-group proposal into a binding community decision (47/52 in favor) under registry-governance-rules section 8
foundation-board ratified PROPOSAL-CSRP-001 under majority-vote-protocol, elevating the policy to legally-binding obligation for all listed packages
foundation-board ratified PROPOSAL-CSRP-001 under foundation-bylaws-article-7, elevating the policy from community-advisory to legally-binding obligation for all listed packages
package-signing-gate deployed to release pipeline; unsigned release attempts rejected with SIGN-001; 847 pre-policy packages grandfathered under snapshot
three core maintainers formally blocked PROPOSAL-CSRP-001 implementation by registering objections under governance-rules section 9, citing GPG infrastructure requirements as a barrier

dissenting-maintainers translated PROPOSAL-CSRP-001 into a different register through formal objection: re-articulating the policy as an accessibility exclusion for contributors without persistent signing infrastructure
